meta.title: Color Psychology Database for SOTY Event Horizon
meta.author: Cleanlystudio
meta.description: Comprehensive color psychology data for auditing and upgrading the Event Horizon black hole WebGL experience

cultural_associations.western: Danger, love, passion, stop signals, Christmas, Valentine's Day
cultural_associations.eastern: Luck, prosperity, happiness, celebration, wedding color in China and India
cultural_associations.universal: Blood, fire, heat — universally recognized as high-arousal color

physiological_effects.heart_rate: Increases by 5-10 BPM in controlled studies
physiological_effects.attention: Highest attention-grabbing of all colors; detected fastest in peripheral vision
physiological_effects.appetite: Stimulates appetite (reason fast food brands use it)
physiological_effects.time_perception: Makes time feel like it passes slower

web_design_use_cases: CTA buttons (increases click-through by 21% vs green in some A/B tests)

famous_examples.usage: Play button, brand identity — energy and action
Full brand identity — excitement and energy

cultural_associations.western: Halloween, autumn, warmth, creativity, affordable brands
cultural_associations.eastern: Sacred color in Hinduism and Buddhism, courage in Japan
cultural_associations.universal: Sunset, fire, warmth — associated with transition and change

physiological_effects.attention: High visibility — used in safety vests and traffic cones
physiological_effects.arousal: Stimulating but less aggressive than red

web_design_use_cases: Accretion disk glow in space visualizations

cultural_associations.eastern: Imperial color in China, sacred in Hinduism, courage in Japan
cultural_associations.universal: Sun, light, gold — associated with illumination and knowledge

physiological_effects.attention: Highest visibility color at distance; first color the eye processes
physiological_effects.arousal: Stimulates mental activity and nervous system

cultural_associations.western: Nature, go signals, money, St. Patrick's Day, environmentalism
cultural_associations.eastern: Islam (sacred), fertility, new beginnings, eternity in Japan
cultural_associations.universal: Vegetation, life, growth — universally associated with nature

physiological_effects.attention: Moderate — less attention-grabbing than warm colors

cultural_associations.western: Corporate trust, masculinity, sky and sea, police and authority
cultural_associations.eastern: Immortality in China, mourning in Iran, protection (evil eye) in Middle East
cultural_associations.universal: Sky, water, vastness — associated with depth and infinity

physiological_effects.attention: Lower immediate attention but higher sustained attention
physiological_effects.arousal: Reduces blood pressure and respiration rate
physiological_effects.time_perception: Makes time feel like it passes faster

cultural_associations.western: Spirituality, deep thought, luxury textiles (historically expensive dye)
cultural_associations.eastern: Third eye chakra, intuition, spiritual awareness
cultural_associations.universal: Night sky, deep space — associated with the infinite and unknown

physiological_effects.time_perception: Can distort time perception — promotes timelessness

cultural_associations.western: Royalty (Tyrian purple cost more than gold), luxury, spirituality, LGBTQ+ pride
cultural_associations.eastern: Nobility in Japan, mourning in Thailand and Brazil, wealth
cultural_associations.universal: Rare in nature — signals something extraordinary or supernatural

physiological_effects.attention: Draws attention through rarity — unusual in natural environments
physiological_effects.time_perception: Promotes sense of otherworldliness and timelessness

event_horizon_specific.role: Primary accent color for cosmic and spiritual elements

cultural_associations.western: Death, mourning, elegance, formality, rebellion, luxury fashion
cultural_associations.eastern: Career and knowledge in feng shui, stability, power in Japan
cultural_associations.universal: Night, void, absence of light — primal association with the unknown

physiological_effects.heart_rate: Context-dependent — can increase (fear) or maintain (calm sophistication)
physiological_effects.attention: Creates focal points when used with contrasting elements
physiological_effects.arousal: Depends on context — can be both calming (night) and unsettling (void)
physiological_effects.appetite: Neutral — often used in premium dining
physiological_effects.time_perception: Can feel timeless or oppressive depending on context

famous_examples.usage: Product and dark mode — premium sophistication

event_horizon_specific.role: The dominant color — represents the void, the singularity, the unknown
usage: Deep space background (#050505 to #0A0A0A — never pure black for background)
The absence that makes light meaningful
design_rules: Never use pure #000000 for backgrounds — use #050505 to #0A0A0A for subtle depth
Pure black (#000000) reserved ONLY for the singularity center
Layer multiple near-black values for depth perception
Use subtle noise texture (0.5-1% opacity) to prevent banding on dark gradients
OLED-safe: pure black areas will look 'dead' on OLED — intentional for singularity

cultural_associations.eastern: Death and mourning in China and Japan, purity in India
cultural_associations.universal: Light, snow, clouds — associated with transcendence and the sacred

physiological_effects.attention: High contrast against dark — draws eye immediately
physiological_effects.arousal: Can be calming (soft white) or overwhelming (bright white flash)

event_horizon_specific.role: Represents singularity, transcendence, the blinding moment of crossing
usage: Final chapter — crossing the event horizon flash
Text color on dark backgrounds (but use #E0E0E0 to #F5F5F5, not pure white)
The 'other side' of the black hole
design_rules: Never use pure #FFFFFF for body text on dark backgrounds — too harsh, use #E0E0E0 or #F0F0F0
Pure white reserved for light sources, stars, and the singularity flash
Use warm whites (#FFF8F0) for organic/warm moments
Use cool whites (#F0F8FF) for scientific/data moments
White flash must be brief (200-400ms) to avoid eye strain

cultural_associations.western: Achievement, trophies, luxury, 50th anniversary, premium tier
cultural_associations.eastern: Divinity in Hinduism and Buddhism, imperial in China
cultural_associations.universal: The sun, precious metal — universally associated with value and importance

physiological_effects.heart_rate: Slight increase — associated with excitement and desire

event_horizon_specific.role: Accretion disk heat, energy, and warmth — the hot matter spiraling into oblivion
usage: Temperature gradient (gold → orange → white at hottest)
UI highlight for key data points

cultural_associations.eastern: Associated with metal element in Chinese philosophy
cultural_associations.universal: Moonlight, metal, reflection — associated with precision and technology

event_horizon_specific.role: Cool metallic tones for UI chrome and secondary information

cultural_associations.universal: Ice, water, technology — associated with digital precision

physiological_effects.attention: High on dark backgrounds — excellent contrast

event_horizon_specific.role: Primary HUD and data visualization color — the scientific instrument aesthetic

complementary.description: Colors opposite on the color wheel — maximum contrast and visual tension
complementary.effect: Creates vibrant, high-energy compositions. Use one as dominant, the other as accent.
complementary.web_usage: CTA buttons against background, error vs success states

examples.effect: Trust meets energy — corporate with warmth
Luxury meets optimism — premium but approachable
Danger meets safety — strong contrast, avoid for colorblind users

analogous.description: Colors adjacent on the color wheel — harmonious and unified
analogous.effect: Creates serene, comfortable compositions. Low contrast, cohesive feeling.
analogous.web_usage: Gradients, background atmospheres, cohesive brand palettes

triadic.description: Three colors equally spaced on the color wheel — balanced vibrancy
triadic.effect: Vibrant even when desaturated. Use one dominant, two as accents.

split_complementary.description: One color plus the two adjacent to its complement — contrast with less tension
split_complementary.effect: Strong visual contrast but more nuanced than straight complementary.

examples.effect: Trust base with warm accents — tech with human warmth

dark_mode_specific.description: Color psychology shifts in dark mode contexts
rules: Saturated colors appear more vibrant on dark backgrounds — desaturate by 10-20% to compensate
Blue light on dark backgrounds creates higher perceived contrast than on light
Warm accent colors (orange, gold) feel more inviting on dark backgrounds
Pure saturated colors (#FF0000) on dark backgrounds cause visual vibration — always desaturate
Text readability: minimum 4.5:1 contrast ratio (WCAG AA), target 7:1 (WCAG AAA)
Dark backgrounds reduce eye strain in low-light environments — ideal for immersive experiences
Use elevation (lighter surfaces = higher elevation) following Material Design dark theme principles
Emissive colors (colors that appear to glow) work much better on dark backgrounds

cosmic_palette.description: Space and cosmic color psychology — specific to Event Horizon
psychology: Deep space blacks trigger awe through perceived vastness
Violet nebula tones activate imagination and spiritual contemplation
Cyan starlight creates sense of cold, vast, indifferent cosmos
Orange/gold accretion suggests heat, energy, transformation — matter becoming energy
The contrast of warm (accretion) against cool (space) creates visual and emotional tension
Darkness punctuated by light creates perceived depth far beyond the screen
Chromatic aberration at edges of bright objects suggests extreme gravity — visual cue for power

deep_space.role: Primary background — near-black with blue-purple tint

warm_colors.psychological_effect: Advance toward viewer, feel closer and larger. Stimulating, energetic, attention-grabbing.
warm_colors.physiological_effect: Increase heart rate, raise body temperature perception, stimulate appetite
warm_colors.web_usage: CTAs, important elements, energy visualization, heat maps, accretion disks
warm_colors.best_on_dark: Warm colors on dark backgrounds create focal points — the eye is drawn to warmth in darkness (like fire in night)

cool_colors.psychological_effect: Recede from viewer, feel farther and smaller. Calming, expansive, contemplative.
cool_colors.physiological_effect: Decrease heart rate, promote relaxation, reduce perceived temperature
cool_colors.web_usage: Backgrounds, receding elements, data visualization, space scenes, ambient effects
cool_colors.best_on_dark: Cool colors on dark backgrounds create perceived depth — they push the background further away

temperature_contrast.description: Using warm and cool together creates depth and visual hierarchy
temperature_contrast.technique: Warm foreground elements on cool backgrounds create strong depth separation
temperature_contrast.event_horizon_application: Hot orange/gold accretion disk (warm foreground) against deep blue-black space (cool background) creates infinite depth perception

white_on_black.verdict: Maximum possible contrast — too harsh for body text

E0E0E0_on_121212.verdict: Excellent for body text on dark backgrounds

9E9E9E_on_121212.verdict: Good for secondary text, passes AA

757575_on_121212.verdict: Borderline — only for large text or non-essential

FF9800_on_000000.verdict: Good for large text and UI elements

CE93D8_on_000000.verdict: Good for accent text and highlights

9C27B0_on_000000.verdict: Fails AA — use only for decorative, not text

dark_mode_text_rules: Use font-weight 300-400 for body text on dark backgrounds (light text on dark appears thicker due to halation)
Increase letter-spacing slightly (+0.015em) for thin fonts on dark backgrounds
Use -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased on macOS for dark backgrounds to reduce halation
Never use pure white (#FFF) text on pure black (#000) — the contrast is too harsh and causes eye strain
Disabled/muted text: minimum #757575 on #121212 (3.5:1 ratio, large text only)

foundations: Dark UI is NOT just 'white UI with colors inverted' — it requires complete redesign of depth, hierarchy, and emphasis
Use elevation (surface lightness) instead of shadows for depth — shadows are invisible on dark surfaces
Reduce color saturation by 10-30% compared to light mode — saturated colors vibrate on dark backgrounds
Use semi-transparent white overlays for elevation rather than gray values — this adapts to any base color
Keep primary surface between #121212 and #1E1E1E — not pure black (feels like a void/hole in the screen)
accent_colors_on_dark: Desaturated accents (pastel-ish) are easier on the eyes than fully saturated
Neon/fully-saturated colors work ONLY for small, intentional highlights (think Tron lines)
Large areas of saturated color on dark backgrounds cause visual fatigue in < 2 minutes
Emissive glow effects (box-shadow with color, text-shadow) work beautifully on dark — they physically cannot work on light
gradients_on_dark: Dark gradients are prone to banding — use subtle noise texture overlay (1-2% opacity) to break banding
Gradient from #000000 to #1A1A1A is barely visible but creates perceived depth
Radial gradients from center create spotlight effect — great for directing focus
Multiple layered gradients create rich atmosphere without any single one being obvious
borders_and_dividers: Use 1px borders at rgba(255,255,255,0.08) to rgba(255,255,255,0.12) for subtle separation
Avoid hard white borders — they look like cracks
For glass morphism on dark: rgba(255,255,255,0.05) background, rgba(255,255,255,0.1) border, backdrop-blur
Divider lines: rgba(255,255,255,0.06) — barely there but structurally important
